name: Finikia (Semolina Honey Cookies)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
1. Cream butter and caster sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Gradually add oil, continuing to beat on high speed until mixture thickens to whipped cream consistency.
3. Sift flour and baking powder twice. Combine with semolina, cinnamon, and cloves.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the creamed mixture alternately with orange juice.
5. Knead the dough until firm.
6. Shape tablespoonfuls of dough into ovals on ungreased baking sheets.
7. Pinch the ends to form torpedo shapes.
8. Bake in a moderate oven for 25 minutes or until golden brown and crisp.
9. Cool on baking sheets.
10. In a pan, stir water and sugar over heat until sugar dissolves.
11. Add honey, cinnamon bark, and lemon juice; bring to boil. Boil over medium heat for 10 minutes then remove the cinnamon.
12. Dip cookies in syrup three at a time, turn them over, and place on a rack above a dish. Repeat until all cookies are dipped.
13. Sprinkle sesame seeds or chopped walnuts on cooled cookies before serving.
From: "The Complete Middle East Cookbook" by Tess Mallos
ISBN: 1-86302-069-1
